page: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the DPDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 614 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. It lacks a dedicated GPU and does not support x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, or AMX.

Performance benchmarks indicate a capable multi-threaded profile alongside some single-core limitations. The instance achieves average multi-core results, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28887.7 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9559.0. Memory read bandwidth is measured at 1125.92 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 129.87 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1108.0 and a weak Passmark CPU encryption score of 2591.19. Large-model LLM prompt processing also falls into the lower performance tier, though text generation speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a high physical core density and lower power consumption profile, offering a favorable resource-to-cost ratio for parallelized workloads. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-threaded execution speed and the absence of specialized vector or matrix math extensions. This server is best suited for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and applications optimized for the ARM64 ecosystem.
description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine within the DPDSv5 family, powered by a 16-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, 614 GB of local storage, and is Premium Storage capable. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28887.7, and strong memory bandwidth. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with a weak Geekbench single-core score of 1108.0 and lower encryption speeds. Lacking x86 vector extensions and a GPU, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose ARM64 applications, web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16pds_v5 is a 16-core ARM64 virtual machine with 64 GB RAM and 614 GB local storage, designed for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine offering dedicated physical cores, balanced memory, and local storage for efficient multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
64 GB of system memory and 614 GB of local storage
Average multi-core performance with 28887.7 stress-ng score
Tradeoffs include lower single-core performance and lack of AVX/AMX extensions
Well-suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
